rationale: The citing paper’s note emphasizes a methodological contribution (ghost/predicted ghost ads for ATET/LATE under auction delivery), while the cited paper focuses on competitive spillovers and frequency effects, so direct methodological dependence is not evident from the notes alone. However, both are closely related online advertising field-experiment papers in the same domain, and the only matched evidence is a DOI with no local citation context. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to classify as an obvious mention-only citation.
